archives.justification: Ignore everything not included in the module itself

exclude.justification: The implementation package is not meant to be used directly, and as such does not need to maintain any backwards compatibility guarantees.
The implementation package is not meant to be used directly, and as such does not need to maintain any backwards compatibility guarantees.
Ignore REST protocol class changes, there were significant ones due to breaking changes to the search API as well as experimental APIs.

differences.justification: Ignore new methods on all types, as these are not meant to be implemented but simply consumed; as such, new methods are perfectly fine to add
The ExperimentalApi annotation is used to mark methods as 'in-development'. It is okay to remove it after a feature is implemented
The ExperimentalApi annotation is used to mark methods as 'in-development'. It is okay to change value field

Updated the parameter type from List to Collection to make API more flexible. This remains backward-compatible for users, as any List already implements Collection and callers can continue passing the same arguments.

This method was previously returning Object, which was a bug. Changing it to String is a more accurate return type and remains backward-compatible, as callers can still treat the returned value as an Object if needed.

TypedPageableRequest was removed from search request interfaces because pagination model selection is now handled via the pagination type parameter in TypedSearchRequest. This provides compile-time type safety for the page() method, similar to how sort models work. The page() method remains available through TypedSearchRequest.

Added a pagination model type parameter to TypedSearchRequest and updated related interfaces (TypedFilterableRequest, TypedSortableRequest, TypedPageableRequest) to enable compile-time validation of pagination method usage. This mirrors the existing pattern used for sort models and search request now explicitly declare which pagination models they support (AnyPage, CursorForwardPage, OffsetPage, etc.).

The page() method signature changed to accept typed pagination models (via SearchPagination

) instead of the legacy SearchRequestPage. The method is now available through TypedSearchRequest with type-safe pagination model parameters (AnyPage, OffsetPage, CursorForwardPage, etc.).
Removal of V1 APIs in 8.10+; compatibility impact accepted for this release.
